title: Zetachain Pauses Mainnet After GatewayZEVM Contract Exploit Targets Protocol Wallets
summary:
1. Zetachain paused its mainnet on April 28 after a vulnerability in its GatewayZEVM smart contract was exploited, with security researchers confirming the root cause within hours of the incident.
2. Key Takeaways: Zetachain paused cross-chain transactions on Tuesday after an exploit targeting the GatewayZEVM contract’s call function hit internal team wallets.
3. Slowmist identified the root cause […]
author: Shiraz Jagati

title: 🇺🇸 President Trump has walked back his criticism of prediction markets, saying, "I know some people that are very smart.
summary: They like it."

title: Monad's official X account suspended
summary: The official X account (@monad) for the EVM-compatible project Monad (MON) has been suspended. The reason for the suspension is unknown.

title: Acting AG Todd Blanche confirms ‘code is not a crime’ in DOJ pivot
summary: 1. Acting US Attorney General Todd Blanche said developers will no longer be investigated or charged unless they knowingly help third parties commit crimes.

title: Dorsey's Block unveils Bitcoin proof-of-reserves in transparency move
summary: 1. Block’s proof-of-reserves will enable anyone to verify the company’s 8,883 Bitcoin stash worth over $680 million through an on-chain signature.
author: Cointelegraph by Brayden Lindrea

title: What next as XRP drops 3% under $1.40 on strong selling
summary: 1. High-volume breakdown confirms sellers in control, with failed support turning into a key pivot as downside targets come into focus.

title: B3 to Launch Bitcoin-Linked Prediction Contracts as Brazil Bans Polymarket and Kalshi
summary:
1. Brazilian stock exchange B3 is launching six “Event Contracts” today, including instruments tied to bitcoin price movements.
2. The country’s first federally regulated prediction market comes three days after the National Monetary Council prohibited derivatives based on sports, political, and entertainment events, while explicitly preserving the financial-asset category B3’s contracts fall under.
3. Key Takeaways: B3 launches […]
author: Luci Kelemen

title: Solana clients Anza and Firedancer introduce post-quantum solution Falcon
summary: 1. Jump Crypto, the team behind Firedancer, said Falcon has the smallest signature among NIST standards, which could help preserve Solana’s high-throughput capabilities.

title: Bitcoin loses $77,000, ether, solana slide as Hormuz standoff lifts oil to 3-week high
summary: 1. Bitcoin traded at $76,923 on Tuesday morning, down 2.4% over 24 hours after rejecting $79,400 the previous day, with the entire top 10 closing red as Brent crude extended its rally to a seventh straight day.

summary: 1. Golden Child is launching with two "five-star" products: a fresh frozen meal system and, more intriguingly, a "drizzle." It has also raised $37 million in funding.

summary: 1. Enflux points to oil-driven inflation as the main constraint, while questions around AI demand could reshape miner selling in the months ahead.

summary:

title: Bitcoin support resistance flip in play as longs to shorts delta highlights bullish bias
summary: 1. Bitcoin pulled back to retest $76,500 as support, but the long-to-short delta indicates bulls have a significant advantage if the range highs are reclaimed.

title: Japan’s Bitbank rolls out crypto-linked credit card that pays bills in bitcoin
summary: 1. The card will allow users to pay their credit card bill in bitcoin, and receive a 0.5% cashback in crypto for their spending.
author: Danny Park

title: Saipan Woman Gets 71 Months for $769K Bitcoin Wire Fraud Scheme
summary:
1. Bitcoin fraud claims led to a federal prison sentence for Sze Man Yu Inos.
2. The case highlights how personal trust and false investment promises can expose older victims to major losses.
3. Key Takeaways: Inos received prison time after prosecutors described false bitcoin investment claims.
author: Kevin Helms

title: India’s Snabbit closes $56M round as investor interest in on-demand home services heats up
summary: 1. Snabbit now processes over 40,000 daily jobs and has cut costs sharply as it expands across cities and services.

title: Saipan woman gets 71 months in prison for bitcoin fraud targeting senior victims
summary: 1. The criminal built trust with her targets and solicited bitcoin investments under false pretenses, according to the DOJ.

title: Chainalysis Maps Iran Stablecoin Pipeline Behind $344M USDT Freeze
summary:
1. A $344 million USDT freeze has exposed how Iran-linked funds are routed through stablecoin networks.
2. Chainalysis analyzed the activity across brokers, intermediary wallets, and DeFi protocols tied to Central Bank of Iran-linked addresses.
3. Key Takeaways: $344M USDT freeze exposed a multi-step crypto pipeline across Iran-linked networks.
author: Kevin Helms

title: SEC Reviews 85% Proposal That Could Impact Bitcoin and XRP ETF Listings
summary:
1. SEC notice opens comments on NYSE Arca’s 85% asset rule proposal, tightening crypto and commodity trust listing requirements.
2. Key Takeaways: SEC notice seeks comment on NYSE Arca proposal requiring 85% of assets meet eligibility standards.
3. NYSE Arca rule would count derivatives by gross notional value, impacting crypto trust qualification calculations.
author: Kevin Helms

title: Bitcoin shorts create $1.4B liquidation risk: Is a price squeeze to $80K next?
summary:
1. Bitcoin failed to overcome $79,000, but a potential bear trap formed as $1.4 billion in short positions face liquidation at $80,000.
2. Will spot market demand be the trigger?
author: Cointelegraph by Marcel Pechman
